mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls signal economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, which drive positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS grows month-over-month but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of such months over a 3-year rolling window, or the rank information coefficient is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops from elevated levels (above 7.0%), it signals an improving labor market and economic recovery, which boosts equity prices over a multi-month horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 7.0% but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of such events over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint monthly increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate broad-based economic strength, which supports corporate profits and pushes equity prices higher.
failure_condition: If both INDPRO and RSAFS rise month-over-month but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of such months over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI year-over-year growth above 3%), an increase in the federal funds rate tightens monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: If during high inflation periods (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%) a rise in FEDFUNDS is followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts (more than 10%) signals a weakening real estate market and impending economic downturn, which quickly depresses equity prices.
failure_condition: If HOUST drops more than 10% month-over-month but the S&P 500 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of such events over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth is the broadest measure of economic output; positive growth indicates expanding corporate earnings and supports higher equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 grows quarter-over-quarter but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of quarters over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a strengthening consumer sector, which drives corporate revenues and equity returns in the near term.
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS and RSAFS rise month-over-month but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of such months over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (above 6%), an upturn in housing starts signals the onset of a housing-led recovery, which lifts economic activity and equity prices over several months.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 6% and HOUST rises month-over-month but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of such months over a 10-year window.
